ocr.text: In the Revenue by Therapeutic Area table for Novartis from 2018 to 2023, the Oncology therapeutic area shows a CAGR of
12.5%, a market share of 25.0%, and features Keytruda, generating revenues of $1.1M in 2018, $1.2M in 2019, with total
revenue at $14.3M, reflecting a growth rate of 6.3%. The Cardiovascular segment, with a CAGR of 10.0% and a market share
of 20.0%, includes Entresto, recording revenues of $800K in 2018, $850K in 2019, totaling $6.3M, with a growth rate of 5.0%.
Neurology, exhibiting a CAGR of 8.5% and market share of 15.0% with Aimovig, reports revenues of $600K in 2018 and
$630K in 2019, totaling $5.0M and a growth rate of 5.0%. Immunology, with a CAGR of 11.0% and market share of 18.0%,
features Cosentyx, generating $400K in 2018 and $420K in 2019, totaling $5.0M with a growth rate of 5.0%.

The Respiratory segment shows a CAGR of 9.0% and a market share of 12.0% with Xolair, producing revenues of $300K,
totaling $3.3M and a growth rate of 5.0%. Pediatrics, with a CAGR of 7.5% and market share of 10.0%, includes Kymriah,
earning $200K in 2018 and $210K in 2019, totaling $5.0M with a growth rate of 5.0%. The total revenue across therapeutic
areas reached $34.0M, with a CAGR of 10.0%. The Deal Type table shows that in 2023, Licensing Agreements had 5 deals
averaging $200M, while Partnerships had 150M; in 2022, there were 4 deals averaging $180M and 120M; in 2021, 6 deals
averaged $250M and 200M; in 2020, 3 deals with averages of $150M and $100M.
